title: Epipactis discoidea
taxonomy: Epipactis discoidea is classified within the family Orchidaceae and the genus Epipactis. It belongs to the order Asparagales. This species is a type of orchid known as a helleborine, a group of terrestrial orchids. The species is native to North America. Epipactis discoidea is often found in woodland areas and is a terrestrial orchid.
uses: Epipactis discoidea, although not widely studied, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's rhizome is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions. In traditional Asian medicine, Epipactis discoidea is used to treat fever, cough, and sore throat. However, more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Further studies are necessary to fully explore the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.
